latest.title: Delay to publication of annual report and accounts
latest.content:
Today 15:01
RNS Number : 4424K
Mirriad Advertising PLC
30 June 2026
30 June 2026
Mirriad Advertising plc (in administration)
("
Mirriad plc

Company
" and together with its subsidiaries, the "
Group
")
Delay to publication of annual report and accounts
Mirriad announces that, as a result of its ongoing administration, the Company will not publish its annual report and accounts for the year ended 31 December 2025 ("FY25 Accounts") by 30 June 2026, as required by the AIM Rules. Should the Company exit administration, trading in Mirriad's ordinary shares on AIM will remain suspended until such time that the FY25 Accounts have been published and posted to shareholders.
Further updates will be provided in due course.
ENDS

latest.title: Appointment of Administrators to Mirriad plc
latest.content:
14 May 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 2427E
Mirriad Advertising PLC
14 May 2026
The information contained within this announcement is deemed by the Company to constitute inside information stipulated under the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) No. 596/2014 4 as it forms part of UK domestic law pursuant to the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) regulations (SI 2019/310) ("MAR"), and is disclosed in accordance with the Company's obligations under Article 17 of MAR. Upon the publication of this announcement via the Regulatory Information Service, this inside information is now considered to be in the public domain
14 May 2026
Mirriad Advertising plc (in administration)
("
Mirriad plc
" or the "
Company
" and together with its subsidiaries, the "
Group
")
Appointment of Administrators to Mirriad plc
On 29 April 2026, the Company announced the appointment of administrators to its UK operating subsidiary, Mirriad Limited.
Following this appointment, it has become apparent that a number of key supplier and employee contracts are held by Mirriad plc rather than Mirriad Limited. As a result, certain liabilities and obligations associated with the Group's business sit within Mirriad plc. The directors of Mirriad plc (the "Board") have therefore resolved to place Mirriad plc into administration in order to protect and preserve value for creditors and stakeholders. Philip Reynolds of FRP Advisory and Robert Ferne of Begbies Traynor have therefore been appointed as Joint Administrators of the Company ("Administrators").
The appointment is intended to support an orderly process in respect of the Company's remaining assets and liabilities, including the realisation of the Group's intangible assets. If creditors are repaid in full, the Administrators' current expectation is that the Company may be able to exit administration on a solvent basis, following which control would be returned to the Board.
Shareholders should note that this outcome remains subject to a number of factors, including the level of realisations achieved, the quantum of admitted creditor claims, and the costs of the administration. T
here is no guarantee that such realisations will be achieved, and should they not, any residual value to transpire from the administration process will be distributed to the agreed creditors and, should funds permit, the Company's shareholders with the Company being dissolved thereafter. The Company's ordinary shares remain suspended from trading on AIM.
As a result of the appointment of the Administrators, the Company will no longer be able to convene the general meeting pursuant to the requisition notice announced on 28 April 2026.
ENDS

latest.title: Appointment of Administrators over UK Subsidiary
latest.content:
29 Apr 2026 16:53
RNS Number : 4606C
Mirriad Advertising PLC
29 April 2026
29 April 2026
Mirriad Advertising plc
("
Mirriad
" or the "
Company
" and together with its subsidiaries, the "
Group
")
Appointment of Joint Administrators over UK Subsidiary
Further to the announcement of 27 April 2026, Philip Reynolds of FRP Advisory and Robert Ferne of Begbies Traynor have been appointed as Joint Administrators of the Company's UK operating subsidiary, Mirriad Limited, on 29 April 2026.
The appointment follows the Company's inability to secure the urgent funding required to enable the Group to continue trading as a going concern. The Joint Administrators are now evaluating Mirriad Limited's position to achieve the best possible recovery for its creditors.
No resolution has yet been reached in relation to Mirriad Advertising Private Limited, the Company's Indian subsidiary, and the position remains under consideration. Further announcements will be made in due course as appropriate.
All of the Company's commercial arrangements had been contracted via Mirriad Limited. Consequently, as a result of the appointment of the Joint Administrators to Mirriad Limited, the Company ceases to own, control or conduct all or substantially all of its existing trading business, activities or assets. The Company is therefore regarded as an AIM Rule 15 cash shell ("AIM Rule 15 Cash Shell").
As an AIM Rule 15 Cash Shell, the Company is required to make an acquisition, or acquisitions, which constitutes a reverse takeover under AIM Rule 14 (including seeking re-admission under the AIM Rules for Companies) within six months of today's date. Alternatively, within such time period, the Company can seek to become an investing company pursuant to AIM Rule 8, which requires,
inter alia
, the raising of at least £6 million and publication of an admission document. In the event that the Company did not complete a reverse takeover under AIM Rule 14 within such six month period or seek re-admission to trading on AIM as an investing company pursuant to AIM Rule 8 (either being, a "Re-admission Transaction"), the Company's ordinary shares would be suspended from trading pursuant to AIM Rule 40. Thereafter, if a Re-admission Transaction has not been completed within a further six-month period, admission to trading on AIM of the Company's ordinary shares would be cancelled.
The Company's shares remain suspended from trading on AIM in accordance with AIM Rule 40, as it is not anticipated that the cash balances available to the Company following completion of the administrations will enable the Company to continue to trade as a going concern. It is therefore anticipated that the Company will be required to raise additional funds in the near future.
ENDS

latest.title: General meeting requisition notice
latest.content:
28 Apr 2026 15:51
RNS Number : 2667C
Mirriad Advertising PLC
28 April 2026
The information contained within this announcement is deemed by the Company to constitute inside information stipulated under the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) No. 596/2014 4 as it forms part of UK domestic law pursuant to the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) regulations (SI 2019/310) ("MAR"), and is disclosed in accordance with the Company's obligations under Article 17 of MAR. Upon the publication of this announcement via the Regulatory Information Service, this inside information is now considered to be in the public domain
28 April 2026
Mirriad Advertising plc
("
Mirriad
" or the "
Company
")
General meeting requisition notice
Mirriad Advertising plc (AIM: MIRI) announces that it has received a requisition notice ("
Notice
") dated 28 April 2026 on behalf of Omar Ahmad, in respect of 1,054,985,000 ordinary shares, representing approximately 6.18% of the Company's issued ordinary shares, requesting the Company's board of directors (the "
Board
") to convene a general meeting ("
Requisitioned GM
") under section 303 of the Companies Act 2006, as amended (the "
Act
").
The Notice sets out six resolutions to be proposed at the Requisitioned GM, being the removal of the Company's three current directors and to appoint three new directors to the Board.
In accordance with section 304 of the Companies Act 2006, the Board is required to call such a general meeting within 21 days of receiving the Notice, with the meeting to be held on a date not more than 28 days after the date of the notice convening the meeting. The Board will make a further announcement in due course confirming the date, time and venue of the Requisitioned GM, together with the formal notice of meeting.
The Company is carefully considering the Notice and
will provide shareholders with its recommendation in the notice of general meeting
. In the meantime, the Board advises shareholders to take no action at this stage.
ENDS
For further information please visit
www.mirriad.com
or contact:

company_30s.what_they_do: Mirriad plc is a tech‑driven advertising firm that builds a platform to embed branded content into digital video streams. It lets advertisers place short, non‑intrusive ads that appear automatically as viewers watch online videos, and it monetises this by sharing revenue with publishers.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company sits at the intersection of video streaming and programmatic advertising, a market that is growing as more content shifts to on‑demand platforms. Its technology promises a higher‑quality, less‑intrusive ad experience, which could be attractive to both viewers and brands if it can scale.
company_30s.current_state: Mirriad is in a survival phase. It has been placed into administration, its shares are suspended, and its annual report is delayed. The company is essentially in a cash‑burn mode, looking for a buyer or a restructuring plan. The market is currently looking at the company as a potential distressed asset.

rns_narrative: The company has been under administration since late April, with its UK subsidiary also placed into the same status. The administration team is delaying the annual report, which suggests ongoing uncertainty about the firm’s financial position.

A general meeting was requisitioned in April, and there have been multiple announcements about the suspension of trading and the appointment of administrators. These moves indicate that the board is seeking to protect shareholders while trying to navigate a possible sale or restructuring.

The latest news shows that the company’s trading remains suspended and that the administration is still working on a plan. No clear turnaround strategy has been disclosed yet, leaving the market in a waiting mode.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders are optimistic that the administrators may find a buyer or a viable restructuring package, which could lift the stock from its current void.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Most of the chatter points to the risk of delisting or liquidation, given the lack of a concrete recovery plan and the fact that the company is already under administration.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has stayed quiet; occasional bullish posts appear when a new development is hinted, but overall the community remains largely on the sidelines.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – there is no public search interest, which means any catalyst could trigger a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: The release of the delayed annual report could reveal whether the company has any assets or cash that could be used to pay creditors or investors.
A formal announcement from the administrators about a sale of assets or a new business plan would signal a turning point.
If the company announces a new partnership or a significant order from a major advertiser, that could provide a confidence boost.
Any lifting of the trading suspension by the regulator would immediately bring liquidity back into the market.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a clear restructuring proposal, a buyer’s commitment, or a delisting notice. A sudden spike in trading volume or a new order from a large advertiser would also be a positive sign.
expect_next.timing_regime: The firm appears to be in the early stages of capitulation – it has already fallen sharply and is now waiting for a catalyst. The APEX score suggests a tight compression, which could mean a breakout is possible if a positive signal materialises.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Neutral – the lack of a clear signal and the presence of administration make a bullish stance risky.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If a credible buyer emerges or the company announces a viable restructuring, the stock could recover to levels that are 50–70% higher than the current trough. This would translate into a large upside if the price rebounds to the mid‑twelve‑month range.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company is already in administration and trading is suspended. Without a clear exit strategy, the risk is that the shares become worthless or are delisted. The worst realistic outcome is a total loss of any invested capital.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 50–70% above the current floor. Potential downside: 100% (complete wipe‑out). The asymmetry is heavily skewed toward loss because of the administration status.

playbook.if_bullish: Only consider entering after a definitive announcement of a restructuring plan or a buyer. Use a small position size, and place a stop 10–15% below the new support level that forms after the announcement.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for the annual report to be released or for a clear asset‑sale announcement. If either occurs, look for a volume spike of at least 50% above the 20‑day average as confirmation before buying.
playbook.if_passing: If the company is delisted or a bankruptcy filing is announced, exit immediately and cut losses.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Mirriad is deeply distressed and under administration; without a clear restructuring or buyer, the risk of a permanent decline is high, so the stock remains a cautious, high‑risk play.
